title: Amazon ups India bet with fresh $13B AI infrastructure investment
slides: Amazon bets $13B on India’s AI. What do they see?
The investment targets new data centers and cloud regions.
It raises a question: Can local infrastructure match global scale?
Your latency drops, but costs may not fall immediately.
Test your app’s performance now to benchmark future gains.
thread_text:
1. What did Amazon see?

A $13B bet on India’s AI infrastructure. Local data centers are the prize.

2. Speed vs. cost

New cloud regions slash latency. But your monthly bill won't drop tomorrow.

3. Scale mismatch

India’s infrastructure can handle billions. Can it match global AI workloads?

4. The real test

Don't wait for prices to fall. Benchmark your app now to measure future gains.

5. Your move

Run a latency test on your current app. Compare it to a mock deployment in an Indian region. See the gap.
video_script: New data centers cut your latency.
But costs may not fall soon.
Benchmark your app before they do.

script: New data centers cut your latency.
But costs may not fall soon.
Benchmark your app before they do.

instagram.caption:
Amazon just committed $13 billion to build new data centers and cloud regions across India.

The investment targets local infrastructure to handle massive AI workloads. Your app’s data could soon be processed closer to Indian users, dropping latency.

Latency improves, but your monthly cloud bill probably won't drop right away.

If you serve Indian users, run a performance test now. How does your app’s current response time compare to a mock deployment in a local region?

carousel_slides.description: Amazon invests $13B in Indian data centers, signaling a massive push for local AI infrastructure.
New cloud regions slash latency, but your monthly bill won't drop immediately.

carousel_slides.hook: Can local infra match global AI?
carousel_slides.description: India's infrastructure handles billions, but scaling for global AI workloads remains a question.
Test your app's performance now to measure future gains when costs drop.
Compare your app's latency to a mock deployment in an Indian region and see the gap.
